tasks.title: Implement reify-syntax parse() with Tree-sitter
tasks.description: Implement the Tree-sitter grammar for the Reify M1 language subset and the parse() function that produces a ParsedModule.
tasks.details: Completed. Tree-sitter grammar and parser implemented.

tasks.title: Implement reify-kernel-occt OCCT FFI binding
tasks.description: Implement the OpenCASCADE FFI binding that implements the GeometryKernel trait for BRep operations needed by M1.
tasks.details:
## Context
The GeometryKernel trait is defined in reify-types/src/geometry.rs. It requires: execute() for geometry operations, export() for STEP/STL, query() for measurements, tessellate() for mesh generation. The reify-kernel-occt crate is currently a stub.

The MockGeometryKernel in reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs shows the expected API surface.

## What to implement
1. Set up OCCT dependency:
   - Use the opencascade-sys or opencascade crate, OR
   - Set up cxx bridge with a thin C++ wrapper layer
   - Add build.rs to handle C++ compilation and OCCT linking
2. Implement OcctKernel struct implementing GeometryKernel trait:
   - Primitive creation: Box, Cylinder, Sphere (via BRepPrimAPI_Make*)
   - Boolean operations: Union, Difference, Intersection (via BRepAlgoAPI_Fuse/Cut/Common)
   - Modifications: Fillet (via BRepFilletAPI_MakeFillet)
   - Transforms: Translate, Rotate
   - STEP export (via STEPControl_Writer)
   - Tessellation (via BRepMesh_IncrementalMesh)
   - Queries: Volume, Area, Centroid, BoundingBox (via GProp_GProps)
3. Handle OCCT exceptions (Standard_Failure) → Rust Result
4. Manage TopoDS_Shape lifecycle safely across FFI boundary

## Interface tests that must pass
- crates/reify-eval/tests/boundary4_geometry.rs: all 9 currently ignored occt_tests (create_box_export_step, box_volume_10mm, boolean_difference, cylinder_volume_query, fillet_edges, tessellation_valid_mesh, translate_centroid, zero_dimension_error, invalid_reference_error)

## Dependencies
- OpenCASCADE must be available as a system library or built from source
- Consider using the 'opencascade' or 'opencascade-sys' crate from crates.io if mature enough
- If not available, set up appropriate build infrastructure
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt and cargo test -p reify-eval -- occt_tests — all 9 OCCT-specific tests must pass.

tasks.title: Implement reify-compiler compile() function
tasks.description: Implement the compile() function that takes a ParsedModule from reify-syntax and produces a CompiledModule with TopologyTemplates.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-compiler — all boundary tests including currently ignored ones must pass. Compile the bracket parsed module and verify equivalence with bracket_compiled_module() fixture.

tasks.title: Implement reify-geometry DispatchPlanner
tasks.description: Implement the geometry dispatch layer that routes GeometryOp sequences to registered GeometryKernel implementations.
tasks.details:
## Context
The GeometryKernel trait and all geometry types (GeometryOp, GeometryHandle, ExportFormat, Mesh, etc.) are defined in reify-types/src/geometry.rs. The reify-geometry crate is currently a stub.

## What to implement
1. Implement DispatchPlanner struct:
   - Holds a list of registered GeometryKernel implementations
   - For M1, just routes everything to the single registered kernel (OCCT)
   - execute(): delegates operation sequence to the appropriate kernel
   - export(): delegates to kernel's export()
   - tessellate(): delegates to kernel's tessellate()
   - query(): delegates to kernel's query()
2. Implement a GeometryEngine facade (optional but useful):
   - Manages GeometryHandle lifecycle
   - Maps handle IDs to kernel-specific state
   - Provides a clean API for the eval engine to call
3. Handle the case where no suitable kernel is registered for an operation

## M1 scope
- Single kernel dispatch (no multi-kernel routing logic needed yet)
- No tolerance budget tracking
- No @optimized hook support
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-geometry — the dispatch planner correctly routes operations to a mock kernel and to the OCCT kernel when available.

tasks.title: Implement reify-eval Engine (eval/check/build)
tasks.description: Implement the Engine facade in reify-eval that orchestrates the full pipeline: parse → compile → evaluate → check constraints → build geometry.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-eval — all boundary tests including currently ignored ones must pass. The full pipeline with mocks and with real constraint checker must work end-to-end.

tasks.title: Implement reify-cli check and build commands
tasks.description: Implement the CLI binary with 'reify check' and 'reify build' commands that drive the full pipeline.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: Build the CLI binary. Run `cargo run -p reify-cli -- check examples/bracket.ri` and verify it exits 0 with all constraints satisfied. Run `cargo run -p reify-cli -- build examples/bracket.ri -o /tmp/bracket.step` and verify it produces a file.

tasks.title: Wire up end-to-end STEP export: bracket.ri → reify build → bracket.step
tasks.description: The M1 exit criterion is `reify build examples/bracket.ri -o bracket.step` producing a valid STEP file. All individual components pass their boundary tests (parser, compiler, eval, OCCT kernel — 121 tests, 0 ignored), but the end-to-end path is not wired up. Closing this gap requires: (1) adding realization syntax to bracket.ri, (2) compiler emitting RealizationDecls with geometry ops, (3) implementing the CLI, and (4) an e2e integration test.
tasks.details:
## What's missing

1. **examples/bracket.ri** — doesn't exist. The bracket source in test fixtures has no geometry/realization syntax. Need to define what realization declarations look like in .ri source (e.g. `realize box(width, height, thickness)` or similar) and add them to the bracket example.

2. **Compiler doesn't emit realizations** — `bracket_compiled_module()` fixture builds TopologyTemplates with params, constraints, and let-bindings but zero RealizationDecls. The compiler's `compile()` function needs to parse realization syntax and produce `RealizationDecl`s containing `CompiledGeometryOp`s (Box, Fillet, etc.).

3. **CLI is a stub** — `crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs` just prints "not yet implemented" and exits. Needs `reify check <file>`, `reify build <file> -o <output>`, and `reify eval <file>` subcommands that wire together parse → compile → Engine.

4. **No e2e integration test** — Need a test that: parses bracket source → compiles → creates Engine with real OcctKernel → calls build() → asserts STEP output contains "ISO-10303-21" and is non-empty.

## Existing pieces that work
- Tree-sitter parser: parses .ri source into ParsedModule (8 tests)
- Compiler: produces TopologyTemplates with value cells + constraints (8 tests)
- Eval engine: evaluates values, checks constraints, calls geometry kernel (24 tests)
- OCCT kernel: creates Box/Cylinder/Sphere, booleans, fillet, export STEP (15 tests)
- All 121 tests pass

## Key design question
What does realization syntax look like in .ri? The language spec may define this. The compiler needs to know how to lower realization declarations into CompiledGeometryOp sequences.

tasks.title: PersistentMap abstraction + im-rs integration
tasks.description: Add the `im` crate to the workspace and create a PersistentMap abstraction in reify-types. Migrate ValueMap from std::HashMap to im::HashMap with structural sharing.
tasks.details:
## Context
M2 requires HAMT-backed persistent data structures for O(1) snapshot cloning with structural sharing. The existing ValueMap in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs` uses `std::collections::HashMap`. This task replaces the internals with `im::HashMap` while keeping the API compatible.

## What to implement

1. **Add `im` crate to workspace Cargo.toml** as a workspace dependency (version 15.x). Add it to reify-types' dependencies.

2. **Create `crates/reify-types/src/persistent.rs`** with:
   - `PersistentMap<K, V>` newtype over `im::HashMap<K, V>` (where K: Clone + Hash + Eq, V: Clone)
   - Implement: `new()`, `get()`, `insert()` (returns new map), `remove()`, `contains_key()`, `len()`, `is_empty()`, `iter()`, `keys()`, `values()`
   - Implement `Clone` (O(1) structural sharing — this is im::HashMap's key property)
   - Implement `FromIterator`, `IntoIterator`
   - Implement `Default`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`

3. **Migrate `ValueMap`** in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs`:
   - Change internal storage from `HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` to `PersistentMap<ValueCellId, Value>`
   - ValueCellId must implement `Clone + Hash + Eq` (check — it likely already does)
   - Value must implement `Clone` (check — it likely already does)
   - Keep the public API of ValueMap the same (get, insert, iter, etc.)

4. **Update all consumers** — anything that creates or uses ValueMap. Key files:
   - `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` — Engine uses ValueMap
   - `crates/reify-test-support/src/` — test fixtures and builders
   - `crates/reify-constraints/src/lib.rs` — constraint checker reads ValueMap

## Memory hints
- Search for `ValueMap` across codebase to find all consumers
- Check `impl Hash for ValueCellId` and `impl Clone for Value` exist
- Look at `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs` for current ValueMap definition

## Exit criteria
- `cargo test --workspace` passes (all 122 existing tests)
- `PersistentMap` clone is O(1) (structural sharing, not deep copy)
- No `std::collections::HashMap` usage for ValueMap internals

tasks.title: Freshness model + Priority types
tasks.description: Update the Freshness enum to the spec's 4-variant model and add Priority/Task types to reify-runtime for the sequential scheduler.
tasks.details:
## Context
The M1 Freshness enum in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs` is `{Fresh, Stale, Uncomputed}`. The architecture spec requires a richer model: `{Final, Intermediate{generation}, Pending{last_substantive}, Failed{error}}`. The reify-runtime crate exists but is a stub.

## What to implement

1. **Update Freshness enum** in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub enum Freshness {
       Final,
       Intermediate { generation: u64 },
       Pending { last_substantive: Option<ContentHash> },
       Failed { error: EvalError },
   }
   ```
   Add `EvalError` type (or use a simple error type) to support the Failed variant.

2. **Add Priority enum** to `crates/reify-runtime/src/lib.rs`:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
   pub enum Priority {
       P0Interactive,
       P1Fast,
       P1Slow,
       P3Speculative,
   }
   ```

3. **Add Task struct** to `crates/reify-runtime/src/lib.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub struct Task {
       pub node_id: NodeId,
       pub priority: Priority,
   }
   ```
   Keep it simple for now — no snapshot or cancellation token yet (those come in M4).

4. **Update all consumers of old Freshness**:
   - Search for `Freshness::Fresh` → `Freshness::Final`
   - Search for `Freshness::Stale` → remove or map contextually
   - Search for `Freshness::Uncomputed` → `Freshness::Pending { last_substantive: None }`
   - Key files: `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs`, `crates/reify-test-support/`

5. **Add reify-runtime to workspace** if not already a member. Ensure it compiles.

## Memory hints
- Search for `Freshness` across codebase for all usage sites
- Look at `crates/reify-runtime/Cargo.toml` and `src/lib.rs` for current stub state
- Check workspace Cargo.toml members list

## Exit criteria
- `cargo test --workspace` passes
- Freshness has 4 variants matching spec
- reify-runtime exports Priority and Task types
- No references to old Freshness variants remain

tasks.title: Snapshot + EvaluationGraph + node data types
tasks.description: Implement Snapshot with provenance tracking and EvaluationGraph with PersistentMap-backed node storage. Build graph from TopologyTemplate.
tasks.details:
## Context
The M1 evaluator uses a flat HashMap of values with no graph structure. M2 requires a proper EvaluationGraph with typed node maps and Snapshot versioning for incremental re-evaluation. This task builds the core data structures that all subsequent M2 tasks depend on.

## What to implement

1. **Add version/snapshot ID types** to `crates/reify-types/src/identity.rs`:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord)]
   pub struct VersionId(pub u64);
   
   #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
   pub struct SnapshotId(pub u64);
   
   #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
   pub struct SourceNodeId(pub u64);
   
   #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
   pub struct ComputeNodeId(pub u64);
   ```

2. **Add SnapshotProvenance** to `crates/reify-types/src/` (new file or in value.rs):
   ```rust
   pub enum SnapshotProvenance {
       Initial,
       Edit { changed: HashSet<ValueCellId>, parent: SnapshotId },
       Elaboration { parent: SnapshotId },
       Resolution { scope: String, resolved: HashSet<ValueCellId>, parent: SnapshotId },
   }
   ```
   Keep it simpler than the full spec — Merge and Import come later.

3. **Implement node data types** in `crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs` (new file):
   ```rust
   pub struct ValueCellNode {
       pub id: ValueCellId,
       pub kind: ValueCellKind,
       pub cell_type: Type,
       pub default_expr: Option<CompiledExpr>,
       pub content_hash: ContentHash,
   }
   
   pub struct ConstraintNodeData {
       pub id: ConstraintNodeId,
       pub expr: CompiledExpr,
       pub content_hash: ContentHash,
   }
   
   pub struct RealizationNodeData {
       pub id: RealizationNodeId,
       pub operations: Vec<CompiledGeometryOp>,
       pub content_hash: ContentHash,
   }
   ```

4. **Implement EvaluationGraph** in `crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub struct EvaluationGraph {
       pub value_cells: PersistentMap<ValueCellId, ValueCellNode>,
       pub constraints: PersistentMap<ConstraintNodeId, ConstraintNodeData>,
       pub realizations: PersistentMap<RealizationNodeId, RealizationNodeData>,
   }
   ```
   For M2, we need ValueCell, Constraint, and Realization nodes. SourceNode, ComputeNode, SchemaNode, and ResolutionNode come in later milestones.

5. **Implement Snapshot** in `crates/reify-eval/src/snapshot.rs` (new file):
   ```rust
   pub struct Snapshot {
       pub id: SnapshotId,
       pub version: VersionId,
       pub graph: EvaluationGraph,
       pub values: PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>,
       pub topology_fingerprint: ContentHash,
       pub provenance: SnapshotProvenance,
   }
   ```
   Implement `Snapshot::from_compiled_module(module: &CompiledModule) -> Snapshot` that builds the initial snapshot from a compiled module.

6. **Implement graph construction from TopologyTemplate**:
   - `EvaluationGraph::from_templates(templates: &[TopologyTemplate]) -> EvaluationGraph`
   - Populates value_cells, constraints, realizations from the template declarations
   - Computes topology_fingerprint as content hash of the graph structure

## Memory hints
- Read `crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs` for TopologyTemplate structure
- Read `crates/reify-types/src/` for existing identity types (ValueCellId, ConstraintNodeId, etc.)
- Search for `CompiledModule` to understand the compiler output structure
- The PersistentMap from task 8 must be available

## Exit criteria
- `cargo test --workspace` passes
- Snapshot can be created from a CompiledModule
- EvaluationGraph stores nodes in PersistentMaps
- Snapshot clone is O(1) via structural sharing
- New unit tests: create snapshot, clone it, modify clone, verify original unchanged

tasks.title: Dependency tracking + reverse index
tasks.description: Implement Adapton-style dynamic dependency traces during expression evaluation, and a ReverseDependencyIndex for dirty propagation.

tasks.title: Content-hash caching + early cutoff
tasks.description: Implement NodeCache with version fast path and content-hash early cutoff. If a node re-evaluates to the same result, don't propagate dirty to dependents.

tasks.title: Dirty/demand cone + sequential scheduler
tasks.description: Implement DemandRegistry, dirty cone propagation, demand cone computation, dirty∩demand intersection, and a sequential priority-ordered scheduler in reify-runtime.

tasks.title: Integration: incremental evaluator pipeline
tasks.description: Replace the M1 simple evaluator with the new incremental engine. Wire Snapshot, EvaluationGraph, caching, dirty/demand into Engine. All 122 M1 tests pass plus new incremental behavior tests.
tasks.details:
## Context
This is the integration task that replaces the M1 evaluator with the real incremental engine. Tasks 8-13 built the components (PersistentMap, Snapshot, EvaluationGraph, dependency tracking, caching, dirty/demand scheduling). This task wires them together into the Engine and verifies the M2 exit criterion.

## What to implement

1. **Refactor Engine** in `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs`:
   - Engine now holds a current `Snapshot` and `CacheStore`
   - `eval()` builds initial Snapshot from CompiledModule, demands all ValueCells, evaluates via scheduler
   - `check()` also demands all ConstraintNodes
   - `build()` also demands all RealizationNodes
   - First evaluation (cold start) evaluates everything — same behavior as M1

2. **Add `edit_param()` method** to Engine:
   ```rust
   /// Apply a parameter edit and incrementally re-evaluate.
   /// Returns the new EvalResult with only changed nodes re-evaluated.
   pub fn edit_param(&mut self, cell: ValueCellId, new_value: Value) -> EvalResult {
       // 1. Create child snapshot with Edit provenance
       // 2. Update the ValueCell's value in the new snapshot
       // 3. Compute dirty cone from changed cells
       // 4. Intersect with demand cone
       // 5. Evaluate only the intersection via sequential scheduler
       // 6. Apply early cutoff
       // 7. Return updated results
   }
   ```

3. **Maintain backward compatibility**:
   - `Engine::new()` signature unchanged
   - `eval()`, `check()`, `build()` return types unchanged (EvalResult, CheckResult, BuildResult)
   - CLI (`reify check`, `reify build`) works exactly as before
   - All 122 M1 tests pass without modification

4. **New incremental behavior tests** in `crates/reify-eval/tests/incremental.rs`:
   
   a. **Parameter change → partial re-evaluation:**
      - Evaluate bracket module fully (cold start)
      - Edit `width` from 80mm to 100mm
      - Verify: `volume` re-evaluated (depends on width), constraints involving width re-evaluated
      - Verify: `fillet_radius`, `hole_diameter` NOT re-evaluated (independent of width)
      - Use a counter or trace to verify which nodes were actually evaluated

   b. **Content-hash early cutoff:**
      - Create a design where changing param A feeds into `let x = ...` but the result of x doesn't change
      - Verify: x is re-evaluated (it's dirty), but nodes downstream of x are NOT (early cutoff)
   
   c. **Freshness propagation:**
      - After full evaluation, all nodes are `Freshness::Final`
      - After param edit, changed nodes transition through appropriate freshness states
   
   d. **Snapshot provenance:**
      - Initial snapshot has `SnapshotProvenance::Initial`
      - After edit, child snapshot has `SnapshotProvenance::Edit { changed: {width}, parent: initial_id }`

5. **IMPORTANT: OCCT thread safety**:
   - Do NOT introduce concurrent geometry kernel access
   - Geometry evaluation remains sequential within the scheduler
   - The e2e_bracket test still requires `--test-threads=1`

## Memory hints
- Read `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` for current Engine implementation
- Read `crates/reify-eval/tests/` for all existing test files and patterns
- Read `crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs` to verify CLI compatibility
- Search for `Engine::new` to find all construction sites
- The test fixtures in `crates/reify-test-support/` may need updates for new test patterns

## Exit criteria (M2 exit criterion)
- All 122 existing M1 tests pass
- Changing a parameter in a loaded design re-evaluates only the dirty-demand intersection
- Content-hash matches on unchanged subtrees prevent recomputation (early cutoff works)
- Freshness propagates correctly
- `reify check` and `reify build` CLI commands work unchanged
